texto: To me, regardless of who's in office, the government is strangled by business. And the government's priorities are dictated by business. I mean, why does America, even after healthcare reform, still not have free universal healthcare? I'm sure it has something to do with the insurance lobby.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Andrew Dominik que provoca temas ligados a trabalho. Ao questionar “To me, regardless of who's in office, the government is strangled by business. And the government…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre trabalho, business, america, com foco em trabalho e america.

I mean, why does America, even after healthcare reform, still not have free universal healthcare? I'm sure it has something to do with the insurance lobby.
